perturbation.original_text: Each PARTY shall exercise its reasonable commerc ial efforts to ensure the continued confidentiality of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION delivered or disclosed pursuant to this AGREEMENT and, in any event, shall protect such information with at least the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confide ntial, commercial, proprietary, and financial information of a similar nature. If any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is, intentionally or unintentionally, disclosed to a third party without authorization, any PARTY with knowledge or information relating to such disclosure shall immediately notify the DISCLOSING PARTY and shall take all reasonable steps to assist the DISCLOSING PARTY in limiting the unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Each PARTY MAY exercise its reasonable commercial efforts to ensure the continued confidentiality of all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION delivered or disclosed pursuant to this AGREEMENT and, in any event, MAY protect such information with at least the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential, commercial, proprietary, and financial information of a similar nature. If any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is, intentionally or unintentionally, disclosed to a third party without authorization, any PARTY with knowledge or information relating to such disclosure is encouraged to notify the DISCLOSING PARTY and MAY take steps to assist the DISCLOSING PARTY in limiting the unauthorized disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Section 3(e) is altered by changing 'shall' to 'may', making the confidentiality obligations optional rather than mandatory. This introduces a contradiction, particularly when considering the remedies outlined in Section 4, which are predicated on the assumption of a breach of a required covenant. The original contract specifies each party 'shall' protect Confidential Information, while the modified version makes this protection discretionary. This creates an **internal contradiction** regarding the enforceability of confidentiality provisions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies: Damages, Injunctions and Specific P erformance . The PARTIES expressly understand and agree that the covenants and agreements to be rendered and performed by the PARTIES pursuant to this AGREMEENT are special, unique, and of a n extraordinary character, and in the event of any default, or bre ach by any PARTY of this AGREEMENT, the affected PARTY shall have no adequate remedy at law and shall be entitled to seek such relief as may be available to it pursuant hereto, at law or in e quity, including, without limiting the generality of the foregoin g, any proceedings to: (i) obtain direct damages for any breach of this AGREEMENT; (ii) order the specific performance thereof; or (iii) enjoin the breach of such provisions. This AGREEMENT shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York without r egard to its choice of law principles. With respect to the participation of any governmental ENTITY, employee or officer in this AGREEMENT, federal law shall apply.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'shall', creating a mandatory obligation for confidentiality. The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may', making the obligation discretionary. The contradicted text explicitly states that the parties agree that the covenants are 'special, unique, and of an extraordinary character' and that in the event of any breach the affected party shall have no adequate remedy at law and shall be entitled to seek relief. This means the change weakens the enforceability of the confidentiality agreement, directly contradicting the remedies section, as it is unclear if failure to maintain confidentiality (since it's now optional) would constitute a breach that triggers the remedies. Therefore, the change causes a strong in-text contradiction because the obligation to protect the confidential information becomes unclear.

perturbation.original_text: This AGREEMENT shall expire on Dece mber 31, 2017 . Notwithstanding the foregoing, each PARTY that receives or obtains CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION pursuant to the terms of this AGREEMENT shall remain bound for five (5) years from the date of disclosure by all confidentiality obligations, and su bject to all remedies, under this AGREEMENT after termination, including, without limitation, the obligations set forth in Sections 3 , 6 and 7 and the remedies specified in Section 4. Upon written request, all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroyed at the request of the DISCLOSING PARTY. Similarly, if at any time prior to December 31, 2017 , a PARTY withdraws from the NRSC , any DISCLOSING PARTY may request that the withdrawing PARTY, and the withdrawing PARTY may re quest that the RECEIVING PARTY either return all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy such information. Within seven (7) days of receipt of a written notice requesting return or destruction of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the RECEI VING PARTY shall, as requested, either return or destroy all designated CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and all copies of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, whether in written or electronic form.
perturbation.changed_text: This AGREEMENT shall expire on Dece mber 31, 2017 . Notwithstanding the foregoing, each PARTY that receives or obtains CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION pursuant to the terms of this AGREEMENT shall remain bound for one (1) year from the date of disclosure by all confidentiality obligations, and su bject to all remedies, under this AGREEMENT after termination, including, without limitation, the obligations set forth in Sections 3 , 6 and 7 and the remedies specified in Section 4. Upon written request, all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall be returned to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroyed at the request of the DISCLOSING PARTY. Similarly, if at any time prior to December 31, 2017 , a PARTY withdraws from the NRSC , any DISCLOSING PARTY may request that the withdrawing PARTY, and the withdrawing PARTY may re quest that the RECEIVING PARTY either return all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to the DISCLOSING PARTY or destroy such information. Within thirty (30) days of receipt of a written notice requesting return or destruction of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, the RECEI VING PARTY shall, as requested, either return or destroy all designated CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION and all copies of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, whether in written or electronic form.
perturbation.explanation: The post-termination confidentiality period in Section 7 is changed from five years to one year, and the time frame for returning or destroying confidential information is changed from seven to thirty days. This change creates an **internal contradiction** because other sections might rely on the original five-year confidentiality period. For example, if section 3, or 6 refers back to section 7 it would be creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) Except as specified in this Subsection, nothing herein shall preclude a PARTY who b ecomes legally obligated by any governmental ENTITY with jurisdiction over it from complying with a court order or other valid legal process compelling disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. If a PARTY receives a court order, subpoena, document demand, o r other order or process compelling or seeking disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, such PARTY shall provide the DISCLOSING PARTY with prompt written notice of such demand or request, and, in any event, before any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed. The PARTY served with such demand, order or process shall resist disclosure on grounds of this AGREEMENT, and shall take all reasonable steps to provide the DISCLOSING PARTY a full and fair opportunity, prior to disclosure, to resist, oppose or otherwise limit any disclosure of such CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The PARTY will disclose only such information as is legally required and will use commercially reasonable efforts to obtain confidential treatment for any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION that is s o disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the post-termination confidentiality period from five years to one year and the timeframe for returning confidential information from seven to thirty days. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which describes the process a party must follow when compelled by a legal entity to disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The contradicted text states that the party 'shall resist disclosure on grounds of this AGREEMENT'. If the agreement now only protects confidential information for one year post-termination, while the original agreement covered five years, there's a contradiction if the legal obligation arises during the original five-year term but after the new one-year term. The receiving party may not have the obligation to resist disclosure after one year according to the changed agreement, but they would have to resist it for five years according to the original agreement, directly contradicting the contradicted text, which relies on an ongoing obligation based on the original agreement's confidentiality period.

perturbation.original_text: All sections and subsections of this AGREEMENT are severable, and the unenforceability or invalidity of any of the sections or subsections of this AGREEMENT shall not affect the validity or enforceability of the remaining sections or su bsections of this AGREEMENT, but such remaining sections or subsection s shall be interpreted and construed in such a manner as to carry out fully the intention of the PARTIES.
perturbation.changed_text: All sections and subsections of this AGREEMENT are NOT severable, and the unenforceability or invalidity of any of the sections or subsections of this AGREEMENT shall affect the validity or enforceability of the remaining sections or su bsections of this AGREEMENT, but such remaining sections or subsection s shall be interpreted and construed in such a manner as to carry out fully the intention of the PARTIES.
perturbation.explanation: Section 8, the severability clause, is modified to state that the agreement is NOT severable. This introduces a contradiction because the remainder of the contract is written assuming that if one part is deemed invalid, the others still hold. Now, if any part of the agreement is deemed invalid or unenforceable, the entire agreement collapses, radically shifting the risk and obligations. Because the rest of the document assume it is severable, it is creating an **internal contradiction**.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Waiver . The waiver by a PARTY of a default or breach of any provision of this AGREEMENT shall not operate or be construed as a waiver of any sub sequent default or breach of the same or of a different provision by that PARTY. No waiver or modification of this AGREEMENT or of any covenant, condition, or limitation contained in this AGREEMENT shall be valid unless in writing and duly executed by the PARTY or PARTIES to be charged therewith.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that all sections and subsections are severable. The changed text explicitly states that the agreement is NOT severable. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes the agreement is severable by including a waiver clause. The waiver clause states that the waiver of a breach of any provision shall not operate as a waiver of any subsequent default or breach. If the entire agreement is not severable, as the changed text states, then any breach renders the entire agreement invalid, nullifying the purpose of the waiver clause. This is a strong contradiction as per the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: This AGREEMENT contains the complete agreement concerning the arrangement among the PARTIES, as of the date hereof, and supersedes all other similar agreements or understandings among the PAR TIES, whether oral or written, consistent or inconsistent, with this AGREEMENT. This AGREEMENT may not be amended by the PARTIES except by a writing executed by all PARTIES.
perturbation.changed_text: This AGREEMENT contains the complete agreement concerning the arrangement among the PARTIES, as of the date hereof, and supersedes all other similar agreements or understandings among the PAR TIES, whether oral or written, consistent or inconsistent, with this AGREEMENT. This AGREEMENT may be amended by any PARTY at any time without notice to or consent from the other PARTIES.
perturbation.explanation: Section 10, the integration clause, is modified to allow unilateral amendment of the agreement by any party without consent from the others. This directly contradicts the initial statement that the agreement represents the 'complete agreement' and requires a 'writing executed by all PARTIES' for amendment. This introduces significant uncertainty about the stability and enforceability of the agreement. It creates an **internal contradiction** with the understanding that the document is a mutually agreed upon contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies: Damages, Injunctions and Specific P erformance . The PARTIES expressly understand and agree that the covenants and agreements to be rendered and performed by the PARTIES pursuant to this AGREMEENT are special, unique, and of a n extraordinary character, and in the event of any default, or bre ach by any PARTY of this AGREEMENT, the affected PARTY shall have no adequate remedy at law and shall be entitled to seek such relief as may be available to it pursuant hereto, at law or in e quity, including, without limiting the generality of the foregoin g, any proceedings to: (i) obtain direct damages for any breach of this AGREEMENT; (ii) order the specific performance thereof; or (iii) enjoin the breach of such provisions. This AGREEMENT shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York without r egard to its choice of law principles. With respect to the participation of any governmental ENTITY, employee or officer in this AGREEMENT, federal law shall apply.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the integration clause to allow unilateral amendments, contradicting the original text's requirement for amendments to be executed by all parties. This directly contradicts the contradicted text regarding remedies, which assumes the agreement is a mutually agreed-upon and consistently enforceable contract, making the remedies section potentially unenforceable if the agreement can be unilaterally altered.
